market_gaps: No product plans a full month of meals ahead AND one-tap-orders it AND hits macros AND caps budget AND spans multiple platforms — nobody marks yes on more than two of these five.
Restaurant delivery has no verified nutrition layer; macro-accurate ordering is unmet.
No neutral orchestrator spends a single monthly budget across Swiggy, Zomato, Instamart and tiffin.
Budget-capped recurring ordering for premium delivery (not tiffin) is unserved.

dimensions.planning_horizon: 30-day plan vs competitors' single-order or weekly pack
dimensions.nutrition_accuracy: Daily-plan macros within +/-10-15% via hybrid LLM + India-native DB
dimensions.budget_control: Hard monthly cap + per-slot envelope + coupon optimization
dimensions.autonomy_model: One-tap approve per order; app-side scheduler (MCP has no scheduling)
dimensions.platform_coverage: Swiggy v1; Zomato + Instamart + tiffin behind one SourceAdapter (v2)

materials: Swiggy Food MCP (14 tools incl. place_food_order, OAuth 2.1 + PKCE)
India-native nutrition DB (INDB free base, Bon Happetee production) + portion-heuristic table
Portable preference graph + spend ledger as the state store

verified_anxieties: Will it order the wrong thing, or something with an allergen, while I'm not looking?
Will an app 'auto-spending' my money quietly overshoot my budget?
Are the macro numbers on restaurant dishes actually trustworthy?

direct_competitors.name: Swiggy native (MCP 'Dietary Planner')
direct_competitors.weakness: Single-platform, no month plan, no budget cap, unverified macros

direct_competitors.name: Zomato native (MCP + memory)
direct_competitors.weakness: Single-platform, order-by-order, no plan or budget logic

direct_competitors.name: HealthifyMe
direct_competitors.weakness: Tracks and coaches but never procures the food — execution gap

direct_competitors.name: EatSure / cloud-kitchen subs
direct_competitors.weakness: Own-brand only, no macros, not cross-platform

direct_competitors.name: Tiffin subscription apps
direct_competitors.weakness: Single kitchen, fixed menu, no macro or budget intelligence

differentiation: The only neutral, cross-platform planner that spends one budget across every food source (the seam platforms structurally cannot build).
A portable preference-and-macro graph that gets smarter with every meal — switching cost the platforms' per-app memory can't match.
Month-ahead planning with a hard budget cap and estimated macros, ordered one tap at a time.
Positioned as a partnership / acquisition target, not a head-on fight with Swiggy.
